content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN elements, such that 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§N1 \le A_i \le N1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§N for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.

Count the number of permutation‚Ä†^{\dag}‚Ä† pairs (P,Q)(P, Q)(P,Q) where:


PPP and QQQ are permutations of length NNN;
Ai=max‚Ā°(Pi,Qi)A_i = \max(P_i, Q_i)Ai‚Äč=max(Pi‚Äč,Qi‚Äč) for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.
Since the number can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

‚Ä†^{\dag}‚Ä†A permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč - the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of permutation pairs (P,Q)(P, Q)(P,Q) satisfying the condition, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.
